restatement: A StreamDock VSD N4 Pro plugin that uses the device's Touchbar Mode (the contiguous wide-display strip) to show the currently playing song and artist from the Apple Music desktop application, occupying the full length of the touch bar.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Target hardware is the MiraboxSpace StreamDock VSD N4 Pro and the plugin is built against the StreamDock plugin SDK (Stream Deck-compatible: .streamDockPlugin folder, manifest.json, HTML/JS property inspector, JS plugin process, local WebSocket transport).
assumption_ledger.rationale: Explicit in prompt; this is the only platform named and matches the documented SDK shape for this device.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Wrong SDK shape means the plugin won't install or load on the device.

assumption_ledger.assumption: The plugin exposes one action whose action type is the Touchbar (wide-display) action, configured to span the full touch bar length.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt explicitly requires Touchbar Mode and full-length display.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If declared as a single-key action instead, the plugin will render in a 1-key tile and not occupy the bar.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Source of 'now playing' data is the local Apple Music desktop application on the same machine the StreamDock software runs on.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Explicit in prompt ('Apple Music Desktop Application').
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If the user expected a web/streaming/remote source, the integration path is entirely different.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Target host OS for the desktop side is macOS.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Apple Music is the standalone desktop app on macOS; on Windows it ships as a preview/app-store app with materially different automation surface. Defaulting to macOS as the simplest, native case for 'Apple Music Desktop Application'.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If user wants Windows, the data-acquisition method (AppleScript/MediaRemote) does not apply and a different mechanism is needed; logged separately as IP1.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Display shows two pieces of text: song title and artist name, on the touch bar simultaneously (e.g., title prominent, artist secondary), and refreshes when the track changes.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Explicit in prompt ('currently playing song and artist').
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Missing either field, or stale text, would fail the named goal.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Polling-based update model (small, fixed interval, e.g., ~1-3s) is acceptable; no event-driven subscription required.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Simplest-within-reason for a local 'now playing' readout; prompt is silent on latency/freshness.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: User notices visible lag between actual track change and bar update; mitigated by a short interval.

assumption_ledger.assumption: When nothing is playing (paused or stopped), the bar shows a neutral placeholder (e.g., 'Apple Music' or blank) rather than going dark or showing stale data.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Simplest-within-reason default for an always-on display surface.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: User confusion about whether plugin is alive vs. nothing playing; low-risk.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Long titles/artists scroll or are truncated with ellipsis so the layout is always full-width and never overflows.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Necessary affordance for a fixed-width display; simplest default is truncate-with-ellipsis, scrolling is a nicer variant.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Either truncation hides info the user wants, or scrolling adds complexity that wasn't required.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Single-user, single-machine, local-only. No accounts, no cloud sync, no auth, no telemetry, no persisted state across runs beyond what the SDK gives for free.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt implies none of these; defaults per charter heuristics.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Only matters if user later wants multi-device or remote; not in scope.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Deliverable is a runnable/installable plugin folder bundle (the `.streamDockPlugin` directory) that the user can drop into the StreamDock software's plugin directory or double-click to install.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Execution notes state the deliverable must be the actual plugin folder, runnable or at least installable.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If user expected source-only or a different packaging, they cannot install it.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Property inspector is minimal — at most a couple of user-facing settings (e.g., polling interval, scroll-vs-truncate, optional show/hide artist), or possibly none.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Simplest-within-reason; the action's job is fixed by the prompt.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: User wants more configurability; easy to extend later.

assumption_ledger.assumption: No album art rendering in the initial build — text-only (song + artist).
assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt names song and artist only. Album art requires extracting image data from Apple Music, which is materially more work and unstated.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: User wanted art alongside text; logged as IP2.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Touching/tapping the touch bar performs no action (or, at most, a benign default such as play/pause). The primary purpose is display.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt frames the feature as 'display' the song/artist; interaction is silent. Simplest default: no-op or trivial play/pause toggle.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: User expected richer controls (next/prev/scrub); easy to add later, logged as IP3.

inflection_points.topic: Host OS for Apple Music data source
choices: macOS only — use AppleScript or MediaRemote/MusicKit-style local query to fetch current track from the Music app
Windows only — use the Apple Music preview app on Windows, which requires a different (and less mature) mechanism such as Windows SMTC / UI automation
inflection_points.why_inflection: Cascade depth and option asymmetry: the data-acquisition layer, the language/runtime helpers, and the install/packaging notes all change with OS. Picking the simpler, native case (macOS) keeps the build coherent; the prompt does not name an OS but Apple Music's flagship desktop client is macOS.

inflection_points.topic: Album art on the touch bar
choices: Text-only (song + artist) — what the prompt literally names
Text + album art rendered onto the touch bar background
inflection_points.why_inflection: UX surface and option asymmetry: adding art requires fetching image data from Apple Music plus drawing/compositing logic; omitting it preserves the prompt's literal scope. Reversible: art can be added in a follow-up.

choices: Display-only (no tap behavior, or a single benign play/pause)
Interactive (play/pause + skip + previous mapped along the bar)
inflection_points.why_inflection: Reversibility is high (easy to add later) and the prompt frames the feature as 'display'. Worth recording because it's an obvious extension the user may have implicitly wanted.

out_of_scope: Streaming sources other than the local Apple Music desktop app (Spotify, web players, etc.)
Lyrics display, queue display, or playback history
Rich playback controls — skip, previous, scrub, like/dislike (see IP3)
Multi-user, account, cloud sync, or remote control of another machine's Apple Music
Mobile / iOS / Apple Music on iPhone or iPad as a data source
Persistent history of played tracks, scrobbling, or analytics/telemetry
Theming/skinning system or user-customizable typography beyond minimal property-inspector settings
Support for other StreamDock hardware models beyond the VSD N4 Pro touch bar
Localization / i18n of the plugin UI
